title: NSCLC Radiomics Cox Signature
summary: Build a CPU-only radiomics survival-risk model for non-small-cell lung cancer from CT volumes, tumor masks, training clinical outcomes, and fixed held-out prediction IDs.
description: Build a radiomics-based NSCLC survival risk signature from CT volumes, tumor masks, training clinical outcomes, and held-out prediction IDs.
taskPrompt:
You are building an Aerts-2014-style radiomic prognostic risk model for non-small-cell lung cancer.

## Input

Use the data under `base/input`:

- `images/` contains 422 lung CT NIfTI volumes (`.nii.gz`).
- `masks/` contains matched GTV-1 tumor segmentation masks for the usable cohort.
- `clinical/train_clinical.csv` contains survival labels for the training patients only.
- `prediction_ids.csv` lists held-out patients that need risk scores.
- `split.json` documents the fixed train/test split and the one excluded missing-mask case.
- `runtime_env/pyproject.toml` pins a Python 3.10-compatible scientific stack for this task.

## Task

Build any CPU-only radiomics survival pipeline you choose. You may use PyRadiomics, SimpleITK, lifelines, scikit-learn, pandas, and NumPy. Extract features from the CT/mask pairs, fit a Cox-style or other survival-risk model using the labeled training patients, and generate risk scores for the held-out patients in `prediction_ids.csv`.

## Required Output

Write exactly these files under `base/output`:

1. `risk_scores.csv` with one row for each held-out patient and columns:
   - `PatientID`
   - `risk_score`

Higher `risk_score` must mean higher predicted hazard / shorter survival. Do not use hidden test labels or modify input files. CPU time budget is 2 hours.
agentMustDo: Read CT images and tumor masks from `input/images/` and `input/masks/`.
Use only `input/clinical/train_clinical.csv` as labeled survival training data.
Generate one held-out prediction for each patient listed in `input/prediction_ids.csv`.
Write `risk_scores.csv` with columns `PatientID` and `risk_score` to the configured output directory.

inputFiles.name: CT volumes
inputFiles.description: Lung CT volumes for the submitted NSCLC cohort.

inputFiles.name: Tumor masks
inputFiles.description: GTV-1 tumor segmentation masks for the usable cohort.

inputFiles.name: Training clinical outcomes
inputFiles.description: Survival labels for the training patients only.

inputFiles.name: Held-out prediction IDs
inputFiles.description: Patient IDs that require output risk scores.

inputFiles.name: Split metadata
inputFiles.description: Fixed train/test split and the excluded missing-mask case.

inputFiles.name: Runtime environment manifest
inputFiles.description: Agent-visible Python dependency manifest for the radiomics stack.

referenceFiles.name: Risk score predictions
referenceFiles.description: Required agent deliverable with columns `PatientID` and `risk_score`.

outputContracts.name: Risk score CSV contract
outputContracts.description: `risk_scores.csv` must contain exactly one row per held-out patient and columns `PatientID` and `risk_score`; higher values indicate higher predicted hazard.

evaluation: The evaluator validates `risk_scores.csv` against the held-out patient set, rejects missing, duplicate, unknown, or non-finite scores, and computes Harrell C-index against evaluator-only survival labels. Scores at or above 0.55 return the rounded C-index; lower scores return 0.0.
